perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereto agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information, and Recipient shall have no obligation with respect to any such information which: (i) was generally known to the public prior to the disclosure under this Agreement; 1 <PAGE> (ii) is already known to Recipient prior to October 27, 1999, as evidenced by the written electronically stored records of the Party dated prior to October 27, 1999; or (iii) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Recipient or any person to whom the Recipient discloses such information; or (iv) is received by a third party without breach of this Agreement or any other obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (v) is independently developed by Recipient; or (vi) is approved for release by written authorization of Owner; or (vii) is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement or request of a governmental agency, or disclosure is required by operation of law.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereto agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information, and Recipient shall have no obligation with respect to any such information which: (i) was generally known to the public prior to the disclosure under this Agreement; 1 <PAGE> (ii) is already known to Recipient prior to October 27, 1999, as evidenced by the written electronically stored records of the Party dated prior to October 27, 1999; or (iii) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Recipient or any person to whom the Recipient discloses such information; or (iv) is received by a third party without breach of this Agreement or any other obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (v) is independently developed by Recipient; or (vi) is approved for release by written authorization of Owner; or (vii) is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement or request of a governmental agency, or disclosure is required by operation of law. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any compilation of such information shall be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original section lists exceptions to what constitutes Confidential Information. Adding 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, any compilation of such information shall be considered Confidential Information' creates a contradiction. The preceding text clearly states that individually, the listed types of information are *not* Confidential Information. The addition then declares that if they are compiled together, they *are* Confidential Information, regardless of the previous exceptions. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty about whether a compilation of non-confidential items suddenly becomes confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereto agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information, and Recipient shall have no obligation with respect to any such information which: (i) was generally known to the public prior to the disclosure under this Agreement; 1 <PAGE> (ii) is already known to Recipient prior to October 27, 1999, as evidenced by the written electronically stored records of the Party dated prior to October 27, 1999; or (iii) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Recipient or any person to whom the Recipient discloses such information; or (iv) is received by a third party without breach of this Agreement or any other obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (v) is independently developed by Recipient; or (vi) is approved for release by written authorization of Owner; or (vii) is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement or request of a governmental agency, or disclosure is required by operation of law. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any compilation of such information shall be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating "any compilation of such information shall be considered Confidential Information." The contradicted text (which is the same as the original text) explicitly defines types of information that are *not* considered Confidential Information, and therefore would not be treated as such, even if compiled. The changed text directly contradicts this, stating that the compiled non-confidential information now becomes confidential. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the definition of Confidential Information, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All written data delivered by Owner to the Recipient pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of Owner, and all such written data, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to Owner upon written request, or destroyed at Owner's option.
perturbation.changed_text: All written data delivered by Owner to the Recipient pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of Owner, and all such written data, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to Owner upon written request, or destroyed at Owner's option. However, the Recipient may retain one copy of such data for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that *all* written data and copies be returned or destroyed. Adding 'However, the Recipient may retain one copy of such data for archival purposes' directly contradicts this requirement. Now, a portion of the data is *not* returned or destroyed, as the original provision clearly requires, creating a direct in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All written data delivered by Owner to the Recipient pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of Owner, and all such written data, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to Owner upon written request, or destroyed at Owner's option. However, the Recipient may retain one copy of such data for archival purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that all written data and copies thereof must be returned or destroyed. The changed text adds an exception, allowing the recipient to retain one copy for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the original requirement that *all* copies be returned or destroyed, as now a copy can be retained. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text. Based on the explanation given, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text. This fulfills the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
